00036| * ); 00037| * }; 00038| * ``` 00039| */ 00040| export function useTokenPricesByAddress() { 00041| const [data, setData] = useState(null); 00042| const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false); 00043| const [error, setError] = useState(null); 00044| 00045| /** 00046| * Fetch current token prices by contract addresses 00047| * 00048| * @param params - Object containing all parameters: 00049| * - addresses: Array of objects containing: 00050| * * chainId: number - The blockchain network ID (e.g., 1 = Ethereum, 137 = Polygon, 10 = Optimism) 00051| * * address: string - The token contract address (e.g., '0xA0b86a33E6Fe17E1B1F493f67e37f43e3DC9e456') 00052| * @returns Promise - See type definition above for structure 00053| * 00054| * Note: The prices array may be empty if price data is not available or if an error occurred. 00055| * Always check if prices[0] exists before accessing its properties. 00056| */ 00057| const fetchTokenPrice = useCallback(async (params: { 00058| addresses: Array<{ chainId: number; address: string }>; 00059| }): Promise => { 00060| setIsLoading(true); 00061| setError(null); 00062| 00063| try { 00064| // Convert chainIds to Alchemy Networks 00065| const tokenRequests = params.addresses.map(({ chainId, address }) => ({ 00066| network: getAlchemyNetwork(chainId) as unknown as AlchemyNetwork, 00067| address, 00068| })); 00069| 00070| const response = await alchemy.prices.getTokenPriceByAddress(tokenRequests as any); 00071| setData(response as GetTokenPriceByAddressResponse); 00072| return response as GetTokenPriceByAddressResponse; 00073| } catch (err) { 00074| const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error('Failed to fetch token price'); 00075| setError(error); 00076| throw error; 00077| } finally { 00078| setIsLoading(false); 00079| } 00080| }, []); 00081| 00082| return { 00083| data, 00084| isLoading, 00085| error, 00086| fetchTokenPrice, 00087| }; 00088| }
